name: Actions Pulse MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for GitHub Actions providing DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) metrics and CI/CD pipeline analytics. Enables AI agents to query deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, and mean time to recovery from GitHub Actions data — surfacing engineering performance insights.
use_cases: Calculating DORA metrics from GitHub Actions data for engineering performance agents
Monitoring CI/CD pipeline health and failure rates from DevOps agents
Tracking deployment frequency and lead time trends from analytics agents
Generating engineering performance reports from GitHub Actions history
not_for: Teams not using GitHub Actions (Jenkins, CircleCI require different tools)
best_when: An engineering team using GitHub Actions wants AI agents to surface DORA metrics and CI/CD performance insights — enabling data-driven engineering improvement conversations.
avoid_when: You use Jenkins, CircleCI, or other CI systems. Actions Pulse is GitHub Actions-specific. For general GitHub Actions management, consider github-mcp-server.

auth.notes: GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) or GitHub App token. Requires read access to Actions and repository metadata. Use fine-grained PAT with minimal scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — reads from GitHub Actions API (GitHub API rate limits apply)
pricing.notes: Free open source. GitHub API rate limits apply (5000 req/hr with PAT).

performance.rate_limits: GitHub API rate limits: 5000 req/hr for authenticated requests

known_agent_gotchas: DORA metric calculations require historical data — ensure sufficient Actions history exists
GitHub API rate limits can be hit when analyzing large repos with many workflow runs
DORA metric definitions vary — clarify which calculation methodology is used
Community implementation — DORA metric quality depends on implementation accuracy
agent_readiness.security_notes: Read-only GitHub Actions data. PAT should have minimal scopes. CI/CD data may reveal security-relevant deployment patterns — restrict access to appropriate team members.

security_score_components.security_notes: Read-only analytics. GitHub PAT with minimal scopes. CI/CD metadata is low-risk but keep PAT secure.
